explanation: FALSE POSITIVE. The exploit requires causing fivePillarsToken.transferFrom to return false instead of reverting, but the token address is immutably set by the owner in the constructor with no setter function — attackers cannot substitute a malicious token. The legitimate FivePillarsToken is an OpenZeppelin-based custom token (evidenced by the OZ imports throughout the codebase: SafeERC20, Ownable2Step) whose transferFrom reverts on failure rather than returning false. The unchecked return value is a code quality concern, but the described attack path — "deploy a malicious token where transferFrom returns false" — is architecturally unreachable because fivePillarsToken is project-controlled trusted infrastructure with no attacker-accessible modification path.
